title: Searching for Files
drDigitalIntro: Can't remember where you saved something? Use Finder's search. Click the search icon in the top right of Finder and type what you're looking for — a file name, part of a name, or even when it was created. Finder searches through all your folders and shows you matches. This is faster than digging through folders manually.

playgroundTask.question: You saved a file called 'Party_Invite_2026.docx' but you can't find it. What do you type in the Finder search box?

drDigitalSuccess: Exactly! Typing 'party' will find any file with that word in the name, including Party_Invite_2026.docx.
drDigitalHint: Search for a unique word from the file name — something that won't match hundreds of other files.
